page.title: Mangusta 80 vs Azimut 58: Which Yacht to Charter in Marbella

data.meta_description: Mangusta 80 vs Azimut 58 in Marbella: compare size, speed, guest capacity and price from €4,719 for 4h. Book your Puerto Banús charter via WhatsApp today.
data.summary: Compares the 24m Mangusta 80 and the 17.6m Azimut 58 Flybridge for charter in Marbella, covering pax capacity, pricing and which routes suit each yacht. The Mangusta 80 starts from €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum with a free jet ski included, while the Azimut 58 Flybridge is quoted on request via WhatsApp, and both depart from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Mangusta 80: 24m LOA, 12 pax, from €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum charter
Azimut 58 Flybridge: 17.6m LOA, 12 pax, price on request via WhatsApp
Both yachts depart from Puerto Banús and carry the same 12-guest limit
Mangusta 80 charters include a jet ski at no extra cost
Every charter includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance and 21% IVA
Mangusta 80 suits larger groups and full-day events needing more deck space
Azimut 58 Flybridge suits smaller parties wanting a sportier, more manoeuvrable cruiser
Neither price includes extra add-ons beyond what's confirmed at booking via WhatsApp

faq.q: What's the main difference between the Mangusta 80 and the Azimut 58 Flybridge?
faq.a: The biggest difference is size: the Mangusta 80 is a 24m superyacht while the Azimut 58 Flybridge is 17.6m. Both carry up to 12 guests and depart from Puerto Banús, but the Mangusta 80 has considerably more deck and flybridge space for the same headcount, which matters most for groups planning to spend the whole charter on board rather than swimming or exploring coves.

faq.q: How much does it cost to charter the Mangusta 80 compared to the Azimut 58 Flybridge?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 starts from €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum charter, all-inclusive, with a jet ski thrown in free. The Azimut 58 Flybridge isn't on fixed published rates — pricing is confirmed on request via WhatsApp based on your date, duration and group size, so message us directly for an exact quote.

Both the Mangusta 80 and the Azimut 58 Flybridge are licensed for up to 12 passengers, so capacity isn't the deciding factor. The difference is comfort at full capacity: the Mangusta 80's larger hull gives 12 guests noticeably more room to spread out across multiple deck levels.

faq.q: Does the Mangusta 80 charter really include a free jet ski?
faq.a: Yes. Every Mangusta 80 booking includes a jet ski at no extra cost, on top of the standard inclusions of licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance and Spanish IVA. It's one of the clearest value differences against the Azimut 58 Flybridge, where watersports extras are arranged and priced separately.

faq.q: Which yacht is better for a full-day charter in Marbella?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 is the stronger pick for a full day out, since its size gives guests room to move between shaded and open areas over several hours, and the included jet ski keeps everyone entertained at anchor. The Azimut 58 Flybridge works well for full days too, but suits groups that prioritise a nimbler, more intimate boat over maximum deck space.

faq.q: Do both charters include a skipper and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, every charter on both yachts includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, full insurance, safety equipment and 21% Spanish IVA. There are no hidden extras to add before you step aboard at Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Where do the Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge depart from?
faq.a: Both yachts depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's main marina for luxury charters. From there, both can reach Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile within a short cruise, with Estepona and Sotogrande also reachable on longer bookings.

faq.q: How do I book either yacht?
faq.a: Message us on WhatsApp with your preferred date, duration and group size. Mangusta 80 pricing is fixed at €4,719 minimum for 4 hours, so we can confirm that instantly; Azimut 58 Flybridge availability and price are confirmed the same way within the same conversation.

data.body_html:
Two yachts, one marina, very different feel on board: the Mangusta 80 is a 24-metre superyacht from €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum charter with a free jet ski included, while the Azimut 58 Flybridge is a 17.6-metre flybridge cruiser priced on request via WhatsApp. Both carry up to 12 guests and depart from Puerto Banús, so the real decision comes down to scale, budget and how you want to spend the day. This guide breaks down both yachts so you can book the right one first time.

Mangusta 80 at a Glance

The Mangusta 80 is a 24-metre superyacht taking up to 12 guests, departing from Puerto Banús. It sits in our Tier B pricing bracket: charters start from €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum, and every booking includes a jet ski at no extra cost. At this length, 12 guests have genuine room to spread across the flybridge, aft deck and interior without the boat feeling full, which is the main reason groups planning a party, celebration or all-day gathering tend to choose it over a smaller cruiser.

  • Length: 24m
  • Capacity: 12 passengers
  • Departs: Puerto Banús
  • From: €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum charter
  • Jet ski: included free with every charter

Azimut 58 Flybridge at a Glance

The Azimut 58 Flybridge is a 17.6-metre flybridge motor yacht, also licensed for up to 12 passengers and also departing from Puerto Banús. Pricing isn't fixed online for this boat — message us on WhatsApp with your date and group size and we'll confirm the rate directly. It's a sensible middle-ground choice for guests who want flybridge cruising without stepping up to a full 24m superyacht.

  • Length: 17.6m
  • Capacity: 12 passengers
  • Departs: Puerto Banús
  • Price: on request via WhatsApp

Size Compared: 24m vs 17.6m

The Mangusta 80 is 6.4 metres longer than the Azimut 58 Flybridge — a meaningful jump in on-board space even though both boats carry the same 12 guests. That extra length shows up most on the flybridge and aft deck, where the Mangusta 80 can comfortably split a group between sunbathing, dining and a shaded lounge area at the same time. The Azimut 58 Flybridge is more compact, which suits smaller parties or anyone who prefers a boat that feels intimate rather than expansive, without losing the flybridge layout both yachts share.

Passenger Capacity and On-Board Comfort

Capacity is identical on paper: both yachts are licensed for up to 12 passengers. In practice, comfort at full capacity differs. On the Mangusta 80, 12 guests spread across a much larger hull, so the boat rarely feels crowded even with everyone aboard. On the Azimut 58 Flybridge, 12 guests fill the available space more tightly, which works fine for a lively group day out but leaves less room to spread out over a long charter. If your group is closer to 6–8 people, the size difference matters less and the Azimut 58 Flybridge becomes the more efficient choice.

Pricing: Mangusta 80 vs Azimut 58 Flybridge

The Mangusta 80 has a published starting rate: €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum charter, all-inclusive, with a jet ski provided free. The Azimut 58 Flybridge doesn't have a fixed online rate — pricing is confirmed on request via WhatsApp once we know your date, duration and group size. Both charters include the same inclusions regardless of yacht: licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment and Spanish IVA at 21%. Compare both against the rest of the fleet on our Marbella boat rental prices page before deciding.

YachtLengthCapacityPrice
Mangusta 8024m12 paxFrom €4,719 / 4h minimum
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12 paxOn request via WhatsApp

Best Marbella Itineraries for Each Yacht

Both yachts leave from the same marina, so the routes overlap: Puerto Banús out to Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, along the Golden Mile toward Marbella Marina, or further west to Estepona and Río Verde on a longer booking. The Mangusta 80's extra size makes it better suited to anchoring off La Concha or Sotogrande for a long lunch stop with the group spread across multiple deck areas. The Azimut 58 Flybridge handles the same coastline comfortably and suits a tighter loop — Puerto Banús to Cala del Faro and back — for groups who want the scenery without a full-day commitment.

Watersports and Extras

The clearest practical difference between the two is watersports. Every Mangusta 80 charter includes a jet ski at no extra cost, so guests can swap between lounging on deck and riding without arranging anything separately. On the Azimut 58 Flybridge, watersports extras such as a Sea-Doo GTX jet ski can be arranged alongside your booking but aren't bundled into the base charter — worth factoring in if watersports are a priority for your group.

Which One Should You Charter?

Choose the Mangusta 80 if your group is large, you want the jet ski included, or you're planning a full-day charter where deck space matters. Choose the Azimut 58 Flybridge if your group is smaller, you prefer a more compact, sportier feel, or you want a quote tailored to a shorter or more flexible booking. Either way, both depart from Puerto Banús with the same all-inclusive extras, so the decision comes down to size and budget rather than what's included. Browse the full range of options on our Marbella yacht charter fleet page and message us on WhatsApp to lock in a date.

Frequently asked questions

What's the main difference between the Mangusta 80 and the Azimut 58 Flybridge?

The biggest difference is size: the Mangusta 80 is a 24m superyacht while the Azimut 58 Flybridge is 17.6m. Both carry up to 12 guests and depart from Puerto Banús, but the Mangusta 80 has considerably more deck and flybridge space for the same headcount, which matters most for groups planning to spend the whole charter on board rather than swimming or exploring coves.

How much does it cost to charter the Mangusta 80 compared to the Azimut 58 Flybridge?

The Mangusta 80 starts from €4,719 for a 4-hour minimum charter, all-inclusive, with a jet ski thrown in free. The Azimut 58 Flybridge isn't on fixed published rates — pricing is confirmed on request via WhatsApp based on your date, duration and group size, so message us directly for an exact quote.

Both the Mangusta 80 and the Azimut 58 Flybridge are licensed for up to 12 passengers, so capacity isn't the deciding factor. The difference is comfort at full capacity: the Mangusta 80's larger hull gives 12 guests noticeably more room to spread out across multiple deck levels.

Yes. Every Mangusta 80 booking includes a jet ski at no extra cost, on top of the standard inclusions of licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance and Spanish IVA. It's one of the clearest value differences against the Azimut 58 Flybridge, where watersports extras are arranged and priced separately.

The Mangusta 80 is the stronger pick for a full day out, since its size gives guests room to move between shaded and open areas over several hours, and the included jet ski keeps everyone entertained at anchor. The Azimut 58 Flybridge works well for full days too, but suits groups that prioritise a nimbler, more intimate boat over maximum deck space.

Yes, every charter on both yachts includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, full insurance, safety equipment and 21% Spanish IVA. There are no hidden extras to add before you step aboard at Puerto Banús.

Where do the Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge depart from?

Both yachts depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's main marina for luxury charters. From there, both can reach Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile within a short cruise, with Estepona and Sotogrande also reachable on longer bookings.

How do I book either yacht?

Message us on WhatsApp with your preferred date, duration and group size. Mangusta 80 pricing is fixed at €4,719 minimum for 4 hours, so we can confirm that instantly; Azimut 58 Flybridge availability and price are confirmed the same way within the same conversation.
